title: Competitive Intel Compiler - Research
description: Competitor research results including pricing models, positioning archetypes, review scores, and strategic recommendations. Created on first run via lazy DB creation.

Company Name.description: Name of the competitor company being researched.

Domain.description: Company website URL used as the primary research source.

Research Date.description: Date on which the competitive research was conducted.

Pricing Model.description: Pricing structure detected for this competitor.

Starting Price.description: Normalized pricing string for the lowest publicly available tier (e.g., "$15/user/month"). Empty if pricing is not public.

Free Tier.description: Whether the company offers a free plan or free trial with ongoing access.

Positioning Archetype.description: Strategic positioning category identified for this competitor.

Review Score.description: Composite review score aggregated from public sources (G2, Capterra, Trustpilot, etc.), normalized to a 0.0–5.0 scale.

Strengths.description: Top 2–3 competitive advantages identified from product positioning, reviews, and marketing signals.

Weaknesses.description: Top 2–3 gaps, complaints, or vulnerabilities identified from negative reviews and competitive gaps.

Strategic Recommendations.description: 3–5 actionable recommendations for how to differentiate from or compete against this company.

Report File Path.description: Local filesystem path to the full Markdown research report saved on disk.

Researched At.description: Timestamp when the research pipeline was last executed for this competitor.

notes: The plugin searches for this database by name before creating it (lazy creation). Company Name serves as the primary identifier for idempotent updates — re-running research on the same competitor overwrites the existing row rather than creating a duplicate. Review Score is stored as a float (e.g., 4.3) and should be aggregated across at least 2 public review sources when available. Positioning Archetype is auto-detected from product copy and review language signals.
